skill.name: detect-ai
skill.description: Analyze text to detect if it was written by AI. Returns a score from 0-100 with detailed metrics. Use when checking content before publishing or submitting.
skill.summary: Check text for AI-writing signals using HumanizerAI scoring metrics.
skill.author: humanizerai

security_audit.summary: Static findings for shell execution and weak cryptography are false positives caused by Markdown backticks and descriptive text in SKILL.md. The skill does intentionally send user text to a third-party API and references an API key, so it should publish with a clear privacy and credential warning.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Text Submission
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to send the user's provided text to https://humanizerai.com/api/v1/detect. This is the intended function, but submitted text may contain private, confidential, or regulated content.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file clearly instructs a POST request to an external API with the user's text. This is a confirmed network and data disclosure behavior, although it matches the skill's stated purpose.

medium_findings.title: API Key Dependency
medium_findings.description: The skill requires HUMANIZERAI_API_KEY for authorization. This is a legitimate credential use, but users must avoid exposing the key in prompts, logs, or shared transcripts.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The authorization example directly references an environment API key. This is expected for the API integration, but it remains a credential-handling risk.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged multiple Markdown inline-code and fenced-code markers as Ruby or shell backtick execution. No executable script or command invocation is present in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown formatting and examples, not runnable Ruby or shell code. The file is a documentation-style SKILL.md with allowed-tools limited to WebFetch.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Not Supported
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported weak cryptography at the description line, but that line only describes AI detection scoring. No cryptographic algorithm or cryptographic operation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 contains natural-language metadata and no hash, cipher, digest, or crypto API. I found no evidence supporting the cryptography warning.

low_findings.title: Critical Combination Heuristic Overstates Risk
low_findings.description: The combined code execution, network, and credential heuristic is not confirmed. The network and API key usage are real, but the code execution finding is a Markdown false positive.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The dangerous combination depends on confirmed code execution, which is not present. The remaining network and credential behavior is intentional but should be disclosed.

dangerous_patterns.title: External API Request With User Content
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill asks the assistant to transmit the user's text to HumanizerAI for scoring. Users should not submit sensitive text unless they accept the provider's data handling terms.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The external POST request and request body are explicitly documented. This is a privacy-sensitive pattern even though it is core functionality.

dangerous_patterns.title: Environment Credential Reference
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill refers to HUMANIZERAI_API_KEY for bearer authorization. Credential access should be restricted to the intended API request.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The credential reference is explicit and tied to an Authorization header. No evidence shows exfiltration beyond the intended API call.

content.value_statement: Writers and reviewers need fast evidence before publishing or submitting text. This skill sends text to HumanizerAI and returns a score with supporting metrics.
actual_capabilities: Accepts user-provided text for AI detection analysis.
Calls the HumanizerAI detection API through WebFetch.
Reports the overall AI score from score.overall.
Summarizes supporting metrics such as perplexity and burstiness.
Maps score ranges to readable verdict guidance.
Provides basic troubleshooting when the API key or request fails.
limitations: Sends submitted text to an external service.
Detection scores are probabilistic and may be wrong.
Does not independently verify authorship or plagiarism.

use_cases.title: Review Drafts Before Publishing
use_cases.description: Check blog posts, articles, or marketing copy for AI-writing signals before public release.

use_cases.title: Screen Submitted Writing
use_cases.description: Evaluate essays, proposals, or application text when an AI-writing risk score is useful.

use_cases.title: Improve Human Revision Workflows
use_cases.description: Identify text that may need more human editing before it is submitted to a client or publisher.

prompt_templates.title: Basic AI Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Use detect-ai on this text and summarize the score, verdict, and main recommendation: [paste text]

prompt_templates.title: Publication Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this article draft with detect-ai. Explain the overall score and which metrics most affect the result: [paste draft]

prompt_templates.title: Submission Risk Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Evaluate writing that will be submitted for review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run detect-ai on this submission. Provide the score, verdict, and a cautious interpretation for a reviewer: [paste text]

prompt_templates.title: Revision Planning
prompt_templates.scenario: Use detection results to guide editing.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this text with detect-ai, then suggest practical revision priorities based on the score and metrics: [paste text]

output_examples.input: A short product announcement prepared for a company blog.
output_examples.output: The skill returns an AI detection score, verdict, word count, and metric summary. It adds a recommendation about whether human revision may help.

output_examples.input: A student essay paragraph that needs originality review.
output_examples.output: The skill reports the overall AI-likelihood score and explains that the result is only a signal, not proof of authorship.

output_examples.input: A client-facing email draft with polished but generic wording.
output_examples.output: The skill highlights the score range and notes whether sentence patterns or readability metrics suggest further editing.

best_practices: Remove sensitive personal or confidential data before analysis.
Use scores as review signals, not final proof of authorship.
Confirm the API key is configured before starting time-sensitive reviews.
anti_patterns: Do not submit regulated, private, or client-confidential text without approval.
Do not treat a high score as conclusive evidence of misconduct.
Do not paste API keys directly into prompts or shared transcripts.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It sends text to the HumanizerAI detection API and reports an AI-likelihood score with supporting metrics.

faq.question: Does it require an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill expects HUMANIZERAI_API_KEY to be available for bearer authorization.

faq.question: Is my text sent outside the chat?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is designed to submit the provided text to humanizerai.com for analysis.

faq.question: Can the result prove who wrote the text?
faq.answer: No. AI detection is probabilistic and should be combined with human review and other evidence.

faq.question: What metrics does it show?
faq.answer: It can show the overall score, verdict, word count, perplexity, burstiness, readability, and n-gram score.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
